signals.headline: Earlier Context (December 2025):** Powell described the economy as "very unusual," citing tariff-driven goods inflation
signals.raw: Earlier Context (December 2025):** Powell described the economy as "very unusual," citing tariff-driven goods inflation alongside labor market weakness. He indicated goods inflation should peak around Q1 2026, assuming no additional tariff rounds.[1] Goods inflation did spike noticeably in the first two months of 2026.[4]
